question: From the Texas counties with 700-4,600 people entering the state prison system in each fiscal year from three years before the year (within 2015-2023) that has the highest total drug overdose deaths across U.S. states through that year, which had 2020 census population under 900,000? List the county names in alphabetical order separated by `, `. Your response should be in the format [Answer].

1.fact: In the VSRR provisional drug overdose death counts dataset (December 12 month-ending values for 2015-2023, excluding the US aggregate), 2022 has the highest total across states at 109,413.
1.subquestion: Which year from 2015-2023 has the highest total number of drug overdose deaths across U.S. states? (Filter Indicator == "Number of Drug Overdose Deaths", Period == "12 month-ending", Month == "December", exclude State == "US"; sum Data Value by Year; choose the maximum.)

2.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY <node_1 answer - 3> data, 18 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes, including Bexar (4,457), Hidalgo (1,698), Travis (1,649), El Paso (1,347), Montgomery (1,321), and 13 others.
2.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in FY <node_1 answer - 3>? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

3.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY <node_1 answer - 2> data, 9 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes: Harris (3,840), Tarrant (3,070), Dallas (2,519), Bexar (2,420), Hidalgo (1,088), Travis (981), Montgomery (843), McLennan (728), and El Paso (723).
3.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in FY <node_1 answer - 2>? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

4.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY <node_1 answer - 1> data, 9 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes: Dallas (3,520), Tarrant (2,869), Bexar (2,822), Hidalgo (946), Collin (851), Smith (823), Montgomery (821), El Paso (799), and Travis (705).
4.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in FY <node_1 answer - 1>? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

5.fact: According to the Texas Department of Criminal Justice Receives FY <node_1 answer> data, 11 counties had between 700 and 4,600 criminal intakes: Tarrant (3,699), Dallas (3,639), Bexar (3,240), Hidalgo (1,181), Montgomery (1,145), Smith (952), Jefferson (806), Collin (769), McLennan (732), El Paso (726), and Johnson (716).
5.subquestion: Which Texas counties had TDCJ criminal intakes between 700-4,600 in FY <node_1 answer>? (Filter: group by County; count rows; keep 700 <= count <= 4600.)

6.fact: According to the 2020 Census Redistricting Data for Texas counties, among the hop 1 intersection {Bexar, El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery}: El Paso had population 865,657, Hidalgo had 870,781, and Montgomery had 620,443 (all under 900,000), while Bexar had 2,009,324 (over 900,000).
6.subquestion: Among the counties that had 700-4,600 TDCJ criminal intakes in each fiscal year from <node_1 answer - 3> to <node_1 answer>, which have 2020 census population under 900,000?

reasoning_chain: Node 1: Year with highest drug overdose deaths (2015-2023) -> 2022
Derived year range: 2019-2022 (node_1 answer - 3 to node_1 answer)
HOP 3 (d=1, data.gov filter - node 6 depends on hop 2):
Node 6: 2020 census population under 900,000 -> El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery
Final answer: El Paso, Hidalgo, Montgomery
